title: The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phrygian
summary: HenFabl is the General Corpus XML copy of a 1994 University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative encoding supplied from Oxford Text Archive item 0243. The OTA header identifies G. Gregory Smith's 1906 parallel-text edition, pp. 2-327, as its printed source, while the plain text labels itself BASS PR, reproduces Thomas Bassandyne's 1571 title, follows Smith's Harleian odd-page sequence in the U-M XML, and includes at least one stanza present in the Charteris stream but absent from Harleian. The exact construction of that electronic text is therefore unresolved and is not reduced to a direct transcription of one physical source. Smith printed five early streams: the 1569/1570 Charteris edition, Harley MS 3865, Makculloch MS Laing III.149, Bannatyne MS Adv.MS.1.1.6, and William Gibb's 1810 transcript of the surviving Asloan Two Mice. Current scholarship identifies Harley MS 3865 as a copy of Bassandyne's 1571 print. Complete public source objects cached here comprise the unique Bassandyne print, the full Bannatyne main manuscript, the full Asloan manuscript, and the complete 1968 Johnson facsimile reprint of Smith's 1906 text volume.

agents.name: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative
Dictionary of the Older Scottish Tongue

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for HenFabl
entities.description: The DLPSTEXTCLASS XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The XML contains 14 DIV1, 15 DIV2, 424 LG, 2,975 L, 109 PB, and 30 HEAD elements; two numbered lines sit directly outside LG and one seven-line stanza is unnumbered.
Its 109 page markers are Smith's odd Harleian pages 3-219, not continuous pagination of the whole parallel-text edition.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding of The Morall Fabillis
entities.description: The public Humanities Text Initiative electronic text dated 1994, structured as DLPSTEXTCLASS XML and identifying Oxford Text Archive U*-243-A as the supplied electronic source.
notes: The header's creation date 1450 is legacy work metadata and is not evidence for the encoding date or a secure composition date.
Front matter, back matter, illustrations, and Smith's editorial notes are explicitly omitted.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive item 0243 / henfable-0243
entities.description: A legacy COCOA-marked, uppercase machine-readable text made for Older Scottish Textual Archive and Dictionary of the Older Scottish Tongue work, deposited in 1984 and now distributed with a TEI header.
notes: The header says the printed source contains five versions and warns that the electronic text may have been constructed from all five, but staff could not investigate.
The text's N code is BASS PR and its opening title is Bassandyne 1571; those labels do not by themselves prove direct transcription from the physical Bassandyne copy.

entities.label: G. Gregory Smith, The Poems of Robert Henryson, Text—Vol. I (1906)
entities.description: Smith's parallel presentation of the five early Fables streams, physically the publication's second volume and Scottish Text Society first series volume 55.

bibliographic.citation: G. Gregory Smith, ed., The Poems of Robert Henryson, Text—Vol. I, Scottish Text Society, first series 55 (Edinburgh and London: William Blackwood and Sons, 1906).
bibliographic.title: The Poems of Robert Henryson

notes: George Stevenson transcribed and collated all texts for the editor.
Charteris occupies pp. 2-218 left/even, Harleian pp. 3-219 right/odd, Makculloch pp. 223-228, Bannatyne pp. 231-315, and Asloan-Chalmers pp. 319-327.

entities.label: Johnson Reprint Corporation facsimile reprint of Smith's 1906 text volume (1968)
entities.description: An authorized 1968 facsimile reprinting of the 1906 Scottish Text Society volume, preserving the original title page and adding a reprint notice.

bibliographic.citation: The Poems of Robert Henryson, Text—Vol. I (1906), first reprinting, Johnson Reprint Corporation, 1968.
bibliographic.title: The Poems of Robert Henryson

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the 1968 Johnson reprint of Smith's 1906 volume
entities.description: A complete 360-page scan whose PDF includes the 1906 title page, 1968 reprint notice, prefatory matter, five Fables texts, due slip, and terminal blank.
notes: Smith's Fables occupy printed pp. 2-327, corresponding to PDF pages 30-355; the U-M Harleian page sequence corresponds to PDF pages 31-247.

entities.label: Charteris-Lekpreuik edition of The Morall Fabillis (1569/1570)
entities.description: The unique extant small-quarto early print: colophon dated 16 December 1569 and title page dated 1570. The schema's scholarly_edition class is used as the available controlled class for a historical print edition, not as a claim of modern critical editing.

bibliographic.citation: The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phrygian, printed at Edinburgh by Robert Lekpreuik at the expense of Henry Charteris; colophon 16 December 1569, title page 1570.
bibliographic.title: The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phrygian

holding.notes: METS identifies the unique extant copy at the British Library; the exact current shelfmark was not verified.

notes: Collation A1 title, A1v contents, A2-N4 Fables, N4v blank.

entities.label: Thomas Bassandyne edition of The Morall Fabillis (1571)
entities.description: The unique extant 1571 octavo print, announcing correction of omissions in the previous printing. The schema's scholarly_edition class is used as the available controlled class for a historical print edition.

bibliographic.citation: The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phrygian, printed at Edinburgh by Thomas Bassandyne, 1571.
bibliographic.title: The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phrygian

notes: Roman type is used in preliminaries and moralities, civilité type in narratives; two illustrations are present.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland digital facsimile of Bassandyne's 1571 edition
entities.description: A complete 62-page digital object, including bookplate and end matter, distributed through the NLS record and Internet Archive.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 3865
entities.description: A 1571 paper manuscript containing 13 fables, their moralities, two prologues, and 2,968 numbered lines; current scholarship identifies it as a copy of Bassandyne's 1571 print.

notes: Title page, tailpiece, two illustrations, and 14 initials were colored, probably later.

entities.label: Makculloch Manuscript, University of Edinburgh Laing III.149
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century Latin logic notebook whose early-sixteenth-century flyleaves preserve the general prologue and Cock and Jasp portions of Henryson's Fables.

notes: The main manuscript is associated with Magnus Makculloch; the vernacular additions may be in the hand of John Purde.

entities.label: Bannatyne Manuscript, NLS Adv.MS.1.1.6
entities.description: George Bannatyne's 1568 anthology. Its fifth part preserves a non-contiguous selection of ten Henryson fables in a different order, interspersed with other works.

physical_description.extent: 375 leaves in the main manuscript object
notes: Compiled chiefly during the last three months of 1568; the Fables selection is in the fifth part.

entities.label: NLS complete digital surrogate of the main Bannatyne Manuscript
entities.description: The 454-canvas NLS IIIF object for the full main manuscript; the three non-contiguous Fables runs are mapped exactly in the local bundle.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 454 JPEG canvas images at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: Asloan Manuscript, NLS MS 16500
entities.description: John Asloan's early-sixteenth-century anthology. Its historical contents list seven Henryson fables, but mutilation has left only The Two Mice, at fols. 236r-240r.

notes: Many leaves have been lost; completeness claims for the digital object describe the surviving physical manuscript, not its historical contents.

entities.label: NLS complete digital surrogate of the Asloan Manuscript
entities.description: The 628-canvas NLS IIIF object for the full surviving manuscript; The Two Mice occupies canvases 485-493.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 628 JPEG canvas images at 1600-pixel width

entities.label: William Gibb's Asloan-Chalmers transcript, University of Edinburgh Laing III.450
entities.description: William Gibb's 1810 transcript for George Chalmers of the surviving Asloan text of The Two Mice; Smith printed this transcript because direct examination of Asloan was then refused.

holding.notes: The relevant transcript is cited by Smith as ff. 236-240.

notes: George Chalmers commissioned the transcript; this documentary layer is not the Asloan manuscript itself.

scope.notes: Identifiers, header, structure, and text match; the exact repository transfer event is undocumented.

scope.notes: U-M explicitly names OTA U*-243-A; case, structural markup, and selection were transformed.

scope.notes: The OTA source description is explicit, but its BASS PR label and the exact method used to construct one continuous text remain unresolved.

assertion.notes: This does not imply that every electronic reading was copied from one fixed Smith column.

ten non-contiguous Fables selections at ff. 298r-302r, 310v-317v, and 326v-342v

scope.notes: Complete is provider-relative to the NLS main-manuscript object.

NLS IIIF Presentation manifest and image services

local_copies.label: Complete NLS Bannatyne main-manuscript IIIF bundle

work_portion.label: Three non-contiguous Henryson Fables runs in the fifth part
locators: NLS Adv.MS.1.1.6, vol. II, ff. 298r-302r; IIIF canvases 279-287
NLS Adv.MS.1.1.6, vol. II, ff. 310v-317v; IIIF canvases 304-318
NLS Adv.MS.1.1.6, vol. II, ff. 326v-342v; IIIF canvases 335-368
notes: The start and end URLs delimit the outer span only; the work is present in the three exact discontinuous runs above, with unrelated works between them.
Canvas 279 supplies the fifth-part heading; the first Fables narrative begins on canvas 281, and canvas 287 ends that run before The Howlat begins on the same leaf.
Canvas 335 begins the final run after the end of The Bludy Serk on the same leaf; canvas 368 ends the Lion and Mouse selection before the next work.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, a standard object inventory, and all 454 canvas images at 1600-pixel width.
Independent deep audit verified 454/454 canvas mappings, member paths, byte counts, SHA-256 digests, and JPEG signatures.

access.access_method: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest and image services

local_copies.label: Complete NLS Asloan Manuscript IIIF bundle

work_portion.label: The Taill of the Uponlandis Mous and the Burges Mous (The Two Mice)
notes: Canvas 485 begins the title and text on fol. 236r; canvas 493 contains the end on fol. 240r and then begins the next item.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, a standard object inventory, and all 628 canvas images at 1600-pixel width.
Deep audit verified 628/628 canvas mappings, member paths, byte counts, SHA-256 digests, and JPEG signatures.
Coverage is complete for the surviving 308-leaf manuscript object; it does not restore the six other Fables lost through historical mutilation.

rights.basis: The work was created and circulated centuries before modern copyright terms.
notes: This conclusion does not transfer automatically to modern encodings, editorial matter, scans, or provider terms.

rights.component: U-M digital encoding and site presentation
rights.basis: The U-M item carries the RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States statement.

rights.component: OTA legacy digital encoding and header
rights.basis: The OTA record and embedded availability statement distribute the files under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 Unported.
First published in 1906, before the United States public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: 1968 reprint-specific matter and Internet Archive scan files
rights.basis: The scan's metadata supplies no explicit license; the public-domain status of Smith's 1906 text does not establish rights in any new reprint matter or the scan package.

rights.component: NLS digital images and Internet Archive derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive item supplied by NLS states CC BY 4.0 for the digital object.

rights.component: underlying manuscript object and medieval texts
rights.basis: The NLS IIIF manifest marks the underlying object Public Domain Mark 1.0.
The NLS manifest labels the digital object Public Domain Mark 1.0. Current NLS guidance says faithful digitizations have the original's copyright status and waives residual digitization-layer rights.

rights.component: underlying manuscript object and medieval texts
rights.basis: The NLS IIIF manifest marks the underlying object Public Domain Mark 1.0.
The NLS manifest labels the digital object Public Domain Mark 1.0. Current NLS guidance says faithful digitizations have the original's copyright status and waives residual digitization-layer rights.
The British Library archives catalog licenses descriptive text under CC BY 4.0; no image license is inferred because images are unavailable.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Five early textual streams of different completeness and order
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel Charteris/Harleian full texts followed by separate Makculloch, Bannatyne, and Asloan-Chalmers texts
editorial_practices.description: Smith did not silently collapse the five sources into one diplomatic text; George Stevenson transcribed and collated them, and Smith printed their streams separately.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Yoghs, thorns, ligatures, suspensions, and tilde standing for n
editorial_practices.representation: Modernized character forms and expanded abbreviated values
editorial_practices.description: The OTA header explicitly reports modernization of these glyphs and suspensions by the depositor.
The OTA header identifies all-capitals text; direct comparison shows U-M converted the content to mixed case.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Smith's complete text volume, with five streams, front and back matter, notes, and illustrations
editorial_practices.representation: One continuous Fables text structured into prologues, 13 narratives, and moralities; front/back matter, notes, and illustrations omitted
editorial_practices.description: The XML header states the omissions; the surviving page markers follow Smith's Harleian odd pages, while at least one Charteris-only stanza is present.

evidence.locator: PDF pp. 7-27, 30-355; printed prefatory note pp. vii-xiv, contents pp. xx-xxi, texts pp. 2-327

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for poemsofroberthen0002ggre
evidence.summary: Confirms Internet Archive object identity, title, creator, date, derivatives, and absence of an explicit item license.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Morall Fabillis: Introduction
evidence.summary: Provides current scholarship on Henryson, all principal witnesses and prints, Harley's dependence on Bassandyne, exact source contents, print descriptions, and debate over collection unity and order.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 3865
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 1571 date and Scottish origin, 13 fables, two prologues, moralities, 2,968-line extent, physical description, dependence on Bassandyne, onsite access, unavailable images, and catalog-text license.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, The Morall Fabillis of Esope the Phyrgian (Thomas Bassandyne, 1571), complete digital object
evidence.summary: Verifies title, printer, date, physical completeness of the cached 62-page object, typography, illustrations, and textual comparison evidence.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for NLS Bassandyne facsimile
evidence.summary: Confirms source digital-object identity, NLS attribution, 1571 date, NLS permanent URL, and CC BY 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscripts catalogue, Adv.MS.1.1.6, Bannatyne Manuscript
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, compilation context, physical description, contents, holding, and linked digital object.

evidence.citation: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest for the Bannatyne main manuscript
evidence.summary: Verifies complete digital-object boundary, canvas sequence, image services, Public Domain Mark for the underlying object, and exact visual work ranges.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and page-range audit of complete Bannatyne NLS IIIF bundle
evidence.summary: Verified 454/454 inventory items against canvases; every JPEG member path, byte count, SHA-256 digest, magic boundary, canvas URL, and label matched. Visual inspection fixed the three exact work runs and same-leaf transitions.

evidence.citation: Catalogue of English Literary Manuscripts, Robert Henryson entries
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact discontiguous Bannatyne Fables folio and legacy-page ranges used to verify canvas mapping.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland manuscripts catalogue, MS 16500, Asloan Manuscript
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, early-sixteenth-century date, 308-leaf extent, contents, holding, and linked complete digital object.

evidence.citation: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest for the Asloan Manuscript
evidence.locator: 628 canvases; The Two Mice at canvases 485-493 / fols. 236r-240r
evidence.summary: Verifies complete surviving-manuscript digital boundary, canvas sequence, image services, Public Domain Mark for the underlying object, and exact visual work range.

evidence.citation: Local integrity and page-range audit of complete Asloan NLS IIIF bundle
evidence.summary: Verified 628/628 inventory rows against the byte-identical live manifest; every image member path, byte count, SHA-256 digest, JPEG boundary, canvas URL, and label matched. Visual inspection fixed The Two Mice at canvases 485-493.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland copyright and reuse guidance for digital material
evidence.summary: States that faithful digitizations have the same copyright status as the originals they reproduce and that NLS waives any residual digitization-layer rights; supports separate treatment of originals, digitizations, metadata, and site content.

evidence.citation: ARLIMA, Robert Henryson, The Morall Fabillis of Esope
evidence.summary: Confirms conventional work identity, Scots fable classification, late-fifteenth-century dating, 13-fable and 2,975-line extent, and Charteris/Bassandyne bibliographic identifiers.

open_questions.question: Exactly how was OTA 0243's one continuous BASS PR text constructed from Smith's five separately printed streams?
next_steps: Seek surviving OSTA/DOST production notes or a concordance-generation specification that identifies the base column and every supplied reading.
notes: The Bassandyne title, Harleian page markers, and Charteris-only unnumbered stanza are all directly observed and must remain visible until the construction is documented.

open_questions.question: What is the verified current British Library shelfmark, access route, and digital availability of the unique Charteris-Lekpreuik copy formerly at Britwell Court?
next_steps: Obtain an exact current British Library catalog citation before adding a shelfmark or claiming that no digitization exists internally.

open_questions.question: When will complete public images for Harley MS 3865 become available again?
next_steps: Monitor the exact British Library record and request imaging or reuse information if an image-based comparison is required.

open_questions.question: What item-level U-M production record documents the 1994 conversion from OTA COCOA text to the present DLPSTEXTCLASS structure and its local section labels?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for production documentation while retaining only directly observed transformation claims.

review_notes: Print dates, manuscript dates, transcript dates, edition dates, encoding dates, repository identity, and facsimile dates are modeled as separate layers.

The full Bannatyne and Asloan caches represent their complete surviving provider objects; their Henryson portions are explicitly discontinuous or partial, and historical losses are not hidden by the word complete.
No complete object was silently reduced to work-only pages: the cached Bassandyne, Smith/Johnson, Bannatyne, and Asloan files retain their full physical or provider-defined source boundaries.
No direct Bassandyne-to-OTA relation is asserted. Bassandyne enters the supported chain through Harley's dependence, while OTA's internally inconsistent source labeling remains an open question.
